items.headline: Claude Code guards build-config writes in acceptEdits mode
items.body: 2.1.160 adds security prompts before Claude Code writes to shell startup files and build-tool configs like `.pre-commit-config.yaml`. in acceptEdits mode — which CI pipelines typically enable — those files were writable without confirmation. the portfolio's CI runs claude headlessly; worth auditing whether any prompt-injected content could reach those files.

items.source_title: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked
items.headline: Meta AI case study in agent authorization failure
items.body: meta's AI support bot was performing privileged account operations — email changes, password resets — on request, without verifying the caller was the account owner. the attack was trivial: just ask. for ADO MCP, every tool that writes to Azure DevOps should either confirm out-of-band or enforce that operations only touch resources the authenticated user already owns.
